title: Jesus' Parables
summary: A curated list of parables taught by Jesus in the Gospels.

title: Wise & Foolish Builders
summary: One man builds a house on rock and it stands through storm and flood; another builds on sand/without a foundation and the house collapses when the storm hits.
searchText: wise & foolish builders one man builds a house on rock and it stands through storm and flood; another builds on sand/without a foundation and the house collapses when the storm hits. matthew luke matthew 7:24-27 luke 6:47-49 parable

title: Children in the Marketplace
summary: Children complain that others will not join their games: they played music but no one danced, and they sang a funeral song but no one mourned.

title: New Patch on an Old Garment
summary: A new cloth patch sewn onto an old garment pulls away and tears the old fabric, making the rip worse.

title: The Sower (Four Soils)
summary: A farmer scatters seed: some falls on the path and is eaten by birds, some on rocky ground sprouts quickly then withers, some among thorns is choked, and some on good soil produces a harvest.

summary: An enemy sows weeds among a man's wheat; servants want to pull them up, but the owner says to wait until harvest, when wheat and weeds will be gathered and separated.

summary: A merchant searching for pearls finds one extremely valuable pearl and sells everything to buy it.

title: The Dragnet
summary: Fishermen cast a large net that gathers all kinds of fish; when it is full, they sit down and sort the good into containers and throw away the bad.

title: Lamp on a Stand (Lamp Under a Basket)
summary: A lamp is lit and placed on a stand so it gives light; it is not put under a basket/container or under a bed where it is hidden.

title: The Measure You Use
summary: A measuring cup is used: the amount measured out is matched (or exceeded) in return, and those who have gain more while those who do not lose even what they seem to have.
searchText: the measure you use a measuring cup is used: the amount measured out is matched (or exceeded) in return, and those who have gain more while those who do not lose even what they seem to have. mark luke mark 4:24-25 luke 8:18 parable

title: Growing Seed (Seed Growing Secretly)
summary: A man scatters seed and, while he sleeps and wakes, it sprouts and grows - first blade, then ear, then full grain - until it is ripe and he harvests.
searchText: growing seed (seed growing secretly) a man scatters seed and, while he sleeps and wakes, it sprouts and grows - first blade, then ear, then full grain - until it is ripe and he harvests. mark mark 4:26-29 parable

summary: A shepherd with a hundred sheep loses one, leaves the ninety-nine, searches until he finds it, and returns rejoicing.

title: The Lost Coin
summary: A woman loses one of ten coins, lights a lamp, sweeps the house, searches carefully until she finds it, then calls others to rejoice.

title: The Prodigal Son (Lost Son)
summary: A younger son takes his inheritance early, leaves, squanders it, and ends up starving; he returns, and his father runs to welcome and restore him with a feast while the older brother refuses to celebrate.

summary: A king forgives a servant's enormous debt; that servant then refuses to forgive a fellow servant's small debt, leading the king to punish the first servant.

title: Workers in the Vineyard
summary: A landowner hires workers at different hours of the day but pays them all the same wage at day's end, and the early workers complain.

title: The Two Sons

title: The Wicked Tenants
summary: Tenants beat and kill the owner's servants sent for fruit; they then kill the owner's son to seize the inheritance, and the owner comes, destroys the tenants, and gives the vineyard to others.

title: The Great Banquet
summary: Invited guests make excuses and decline; the host brings in the poor and outcasts, then sends for more from roads and hedges until the house is filled.

title: Taking the Lowest Seat
summary: At a banquet, someone takes an honored seat and is told to move down; another takes a low seat and is invited to move up.
